summary.intent: Implement and iterate on a browser-in-browser feature for the Moltis agent platform, enabling users to view and interact with browser sessions from the UI while agents can also control the same sessions.
summary.outcome: Delivered a fully functional interactive browser viewer with session management, URL bar with autocomplete, screenshot prefetching, scroll/click relay, proper coordinate mapping, and fixed multiple critical bugs including WebSocket subscription gap, screencast relay race condition, and session isolation issues.

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService previously had separate BrowserManager instances — sessions created by the tool were invisible to the UI API until shared via Arc<OnceCell>
The gateway uses a WebSocket v4 protocol with explicit event subscriptions — events not in the subscribeEvents list in websocket.js are silently dropped by broadcast.rs line ~100

serde #[serde(default)] only handles missing keys, not explicit null values — LLMs (especially MiniMax) send null for all optional fields, requiring a retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) strip before deserialization
CDP Page.startScreencast relay exits if tx.send() finds zero receivers (lagged error path) — race condition when subscriber connects after first frame
CDP mouseWheel events require both deltaX and deltaY to always be present, even when zero — omitting either causes a CDP protocol error
about:blank generates no screencast frames from CDP — sessionss starting blank need to navigate before screencast produces output
The workspace clippy config denies .expect() calls even in test modules
Browser containers use browserless/chrome image via apple-container backend with a configurable idle timeout (was 5min, raised to 30min) and TTL (was 30min, raised to 2h)
Screencast metadata includes offset_top (browser chrome height) which must be added to y-coordinates for accurate click mapping
Default viewport was 2560x1440@2x — too large for interactive use; changed to 1440x900@1x

code.finding: Null-stripping fix: obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) applied before serde deserialization in execute()
Race condition: relay task exited on SendError::Closed (zero receivers). Fixed by dropping frames silently instead of exiting when no subscribers.
Per-client subscription filter — events not in client.subscriptions are silently dropped
browser.screencast.frame was missing from subscribeEvents list, causing all screencast frames to be dropped at the broadcast filter
mouseWheel CDP dispatch requires deltaX and deltaY always present; conditional on non-zero was breaking scroll
Single CDP failure immediately marked session as stale and closed it — fixed with retry logic and has_session guard to prevent flood cleanup
Default viewport changed from 2560x1440 device_scale_factor=2.0 to 1440x900 device_scale_factor=1.0 for interactive viewing

workflow: Canvas event listeners attached in useEffect([]) never re-run when canvas is conditionally rendered — use ref callbacks (node => { if(node) attachListeners(node) }) instead
Use offsetX/offsetY with clientWidth/clientHeight (not getBoundingClientRect) for canvas coordinate mapping to avoid border offset errors
Use img.naturalWidth/naturalHeight for screencast coordinate scaling rather than hardcoded dimensions or metadata — more reliable across viewport changes
rAF-gated canvas rendering drops excess frames naturally instead of wasting CPU on every frame signal
YubiKey tap required for every git push in this repo — must retry push manually after each tap
Wheel events throttled at 50ms with accumulated deltas; mousemove throttled to avoid HTTP request flooding from rapid DOM events
When debugging CDP frame delivery issues, add tracing at each hop: CDP → ScreencastRegistry → service subscribe → API relay → WebSocket

friction: YubiKey tap required on every push — caused multiple failed push attempts requiring manual retries

Three separate root causes for 'Waiting for first frame': (1) screencast relay race, (2) navigate silently failed due to missing https://, (3) WebSocket subscription filter — each required a separate debugging cycle
Parallel integration tests fought over browser profile lock files — required --test-threads=1
CSS aspect-ratio hardcoded as 16/10 didn't match actual 16:9 viewport, contributing to click offset issues alongside the missing offset_top correction
Apple Container Chrome processes crashed under memory pressure, triggering aggressive stale session cleanup that flooded logs and killed sessions prematurely
open_items: WebRTC upgrade path identified (neko architecture, webrtc-rs) for sub-100ms latency and 6-18x bandwidth reduction vs CDP JPEG screencasting — deferred as longer-term work
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast (eliminate ~33% base64 overhead) — identified but not implemented
Per-agent browser profiles for isolation — not built, currently single global profile
Browser connection resilience: Apple Container Chrome crashes under memory pressure — retry/reconnect logic not fully implemented, only retry on single CDP failure added
Export cookies button was removed from UI — functionality may need to be re-exposed elsewhere
Playwright e2e tests for browser interaction (clicks, scroll) were added but rely on a live backend — CI integration strategy not finalized

URL polling (2s interval via get_url CDP action) is a workaround for in-page navigation detection — CDP Page.frameNavigated events would be cleaner but require event subscription infrastructure changes
